components.name: accordion
components.description: Collapsible accordion sections with single or multiple open items — animated via CSS grid-template-rows
components.whenToUse: Use when presenting multiple related sections where the reader may expand one at a time. Good for FAQs, reference lists, and grouped details.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for a single collapsible item — use Reveal or Spoiler instead. Do not use for navigation menus.
components.example:
<Accordion>
  <AccordionItem value="q1">
    <AccordionTrigger>What is an invariant?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>A condition that holds true before and after every operation.</AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

components.name: alert
components.description: Semantic alert boxes with role='alert' and info/warning/destructive/success variants
components.whenToUse: Use for system-level feedback requiring immediate attention — form errors, operation results, destructive-action warnings.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for general information or tips — use Callout instead. Alert implies the reader must act or be immediately aware.
components.example:
<Alert variant="destructive">
  <AlertTitle>Error</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>Your session has expired. Please sign in again.</AlertDescription>
</Alert>

components.name: annotation
components.description: Inline text annotation — click an annotated phrase to reveal an explanation popover, with Escape/click-outside dismissal
components.whenToUse: Use to explain a specific inline term, symbol, or phrase without interrupting prose flow. Ideal for technical notation like O(n²) or domain-specific terms appearing once.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for long explanations — use Callout or Definition. Do not annotate every technical term on a page; annotate only where the reader is likely to be unfamiliar.
components.example: <Annotation note="Quadratic time: for each element we scan all remaining elements.">O(n²)</Annotation>

components.name: badge
components.description: Status badges with multiple variants (default, success, warning, info, destructive)
components.whenToUse: Use for status labels, version tags, or category markers that appear inline or in lists.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for interactive or clickable labels — use a button. Do not rely on badge alone for critical information.
components.example: <Badge variant="success">Stable</Badge>

components.name: blockquote
components.description: Styled quote blocks with optional citation
components.whenToUse: Use for direct quotations from external sources, books, papers, or people.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for callouts, tips, or notes — use Callout instead. Not for code output.
components.example: Simplicity is prerequisite for reliability.

components.name: callout
components.description: Alert boxes for important information with variants
components.whenToUse: Use for tips, notes, warnings, and supplementary information. The reader can continue without acting on it immediately.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for system feedback — use Alert. Do not nest callouts. Do not use for quotations — use Blockquote.
components.example:
<Callout variant="warning" title="Breaking change">
  This API was removed in v2.0. Use the new `useCollapse` hook instead.
</Callout>

components.name: card
components.description: Card layout components (Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter) plus LinkCard for navigation grids
components.whenToUse: Use Card for structured content blocks with a clear header, body, and optional footer. Use LinkCard for navigation grids — next steps, related topics.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use cards for flowing prose content. Do not use LinkCard without a meaningful title and description.
components.example: <LinkCard title="Binary Search Tree" href="/docs/bst" description="Self-balancing search with O(log n) operations." />

components.name: certification-badge
components.description: Compliance certification display — renders ISO, TISAX, SOC 2, and similar certification chips with status indicators (active, pending, expired)
components.whenToUse: Use at the top of a compliance, privacy, or security section to establish trust context at a glance. Good for ISO 27001, TISAX, SOC 2, GDPR, and other certifications.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for generic labels or version tags — use Badge instead. Do not misrepresent certification status.
components.example:
<CertificationBadge
  certs={[
    { name: "ISO 27001:2022", scope: "Information Security Management", year: 2023, status: "active" },
    { name: "TISAX", scope: "Automotive Information Security", status: "active" },
  ]}
/>

components.name: changelog
components.description: Changelog component — versioned entries with a timeline and typed change badges (added/fixed/changed/removed/security)
components.whenToUse: Use for versioned release notes with typed change entries: added, fixed, changed, removed, security.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for general timelines — use a Steps or list. Do not use for non-versioned content.
components.example:
<Changelog version="2.1.0" date="2026-01-15">
  <ChangelogEntry type="added">Collapse animation on Accordion</ChangelogEntry>
  <ChangelogEntry type="fixed">Focus ring on mobile Safari</ChangelogEntry>
</Changelog>

components.name: code-block
components.description: Syntax highlighted code blocks with title and line numbers
components.whenToUse: Use for any code snippet needing syntax highlighting, a language label, a title, or a copy button. Prefer over raw fenced code blocks when context matters.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for command-line sessions — use Terminal. Do not use for showing diffs — use DiffBlock.
components.example:
<CodeBlock title="binary-search.ts" data-language="typescript">
  {`function binarySearch(arr: number[], target: number): number {
    let lo = 0, hi = arr.length - 1
    while (lo <= hi) {
      const mid = (lo + hi) >> 1
      if (arr[mid] === target) return mid
      arr[mid] < target ? lo = mid + 1 : hi = mid - 1
    }
    return -1
  }`}
</CodeBlock>

components.name: code-group
components.description: Tabbed code block group — show npm/pnpm/yarn or multi-language examples side by side
components.whenToUse: Use to show the same content in multiple formats — npm/pnpm/yarn install commands, or the same snippet in TypeScript and JavaScript.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for unrelated code blocks — use separate CodeBlocks. Do not use for non-code content.
components.example:
<CodeGroup>
  <CodeBlock title="npm">npm install docsui-cli</CodeBlock>
  <CodeBlock title="pnpm">pnpm add docsui-cli</CodeBlock>
  <CodeBlock title="yarn">yarn add docsui-cli</CodeBlock>
</CodeGroup>

components.name: complexity-table
components.description: Complexity table — structured display of time and space complexity for algorithm operations
components.whenToUse: Use to document time and space complexity for algorithm operations in a structured, scannable format. Place after the algorithm description.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-algorithmic content. When only one operation exists, use Invariant instead.
components.example:
<ComplexityTable
  rows={[
    { operation: "Search", best: "O(1)", average: "O(log n)", worst: "O(n)" },
    { operation: "Insert", best: "O(log n)", average: "O(log n)", worst: "O(n)" },
    { operation: "Delete", best: "O(log n)", average: "O(log n)", worst: "O(n)" }
  ]}
/>

components.name: data-table
components.description: Dynamic data table — accepts columns and data as props so you can pass static arrays, imported JSON, or API responses
components.whenToUse: Use for tabular data with more than 10 rows, or when sorting or filtering is needed, or when data comes from a dynamic source.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for simple static reference tables with fewer than 10 rows — use Table instead.
components.example: <DataTable columns={columns} data={rows} />

components.name: data-type-table
components.description: AI/ML numeric data type and tensor specification table — documents hardware-supported types (INT8, FP16, BF16, FP32) with bits, range, shape, and quantization support
components.whenToUse: Use when documenting AI runtime data types, hardware inference engines, or ML framework precision support. Ideal for QAIRT, TensorFlow Lite, or ONNX type compatibility tables.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for general comparison tables — use DataTable instead. Not for algorithm complexity — use ComplexityTable.
components.example:
<DataTypeTable
  rows={[
    { type: "INT8",  bits: 8,  range: "-128 to 127", quantized: true,  description: "Inference optimised integer" },
    { type: "FP16",  bits: 16, range: "±65504",       quantized: false, description: "Half-precision float" },
    { type: "FP32",  bits: 32, range: "±3.4e38",      quantized: false, description: "Full-precision float" },
  ]}
  caption="Qualcomm AI Runtime (QAIRT) supported types"
/>

components.name: definition
components.description: Formal term definition component — displays a term with its definition in a structured format
components.whenToUse: Use to formally define a term the reader may not know. Place at first use of a specialized term in the document.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for general explanations — use Callout. Do not define terms the target reader already knows.
components.example:
<Definition term="Invariant">
  A condition that holds true before and after every operation on a data structure.
</Definition>

components.name: diff-block
components.description: Code diff block — highlights + additions and - removals with green/red backgrounds
components.whenToUse: Use to show what changed in code — before/after a refactor, migration steps, or patch explanations.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use to show two alternatives side by side — use CodeGroup. Not for prose changes.
components.example:
<DiffBlock>
  {`- const result = items.filter(x => x.active)
+ const result = items.filter(({ active }) => active)`}
</DiffBlock>

components.name: ds
components.description: Data structure visualization components — Array, Matrix, LinkedList, DoublyLinkedList, CircularLinkedList, Stack, Queue, Deque, PriorityQueue, BinaryTree, BST, MinHeap, MaxHeap, Trie, Graph, Digraph, WeightedGraph, DAG, HashMap, HashSet, NaryTree, SegmentTree, BFS, DFS, AgentFlow
components.whenToUse: Use to visually render linear and graph data structures — Array, LinkedList, Stack, Queue, Graph, HashMap, etc. Use the specific named export matching the structure.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for tree structures — use ds-tree components. Do not use for flow diagrams — use Mermaid.
components.example:
<DSArray data={[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]} />
<DSLinkedList data={[10, 20, 30]} />

components.name: ds-tree
components.description: Production-grade SVG tree visualizer — Binary Tree, BST, AVL, Balanced, Full, Complete, Perfect, Min/Max Heap, N-ary Tree. Includes validation engine, layout engine, traversal animation (BFS/DFS/inorder/preorder/postorder), interactive playground, and valid/invalid state rendering.
components.whenToUse: Use to visually render tree-structured data — Binary Tree, BST, AVL, Heap, N-ary Tree, Trie. Includes traversal animation.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for abstract concept diagrams — use Mermaid. Use only when the visual structure of the tree data is the point.
components.example: <MermaidBST data={[8, 3, 10, 1, 6, 14]} />

components.name: electronics
components.description: Electrical circuit symbol components — ElecWire, ElecNode, ElecGround, ElecResistor, ElecCapacitor, ElecInductor, ElecFuse, ElecBattery, ElecVoltageSource, ElecACSource, ElecCurrentSource, ElecSwitch, ElecDiode, ElecLED, ElecLamp, ElecVoltmeter, ElecAmmeter, ElecLabel. All must be used inside <FigScene>.
components.whenToUse: Use to draw electrical circuit schematics in MDX — resistors, capacitors, batteries, switches, diodes, sources. Place inside FigScene with world coordinates.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for logic gate diagrams — use Mermaid. Do not use outside FigScene. Requires geometry-2d as a peer dependency.
components.example:
<FigScene width={500} height={300} xRange={[-1,10]} yRange={[-3,3]} showGrid={false} showAxes={false}>
  <ElecWire x1={0} y1={0} x2={2} y2={0} />
  <ElecResistor x1={2} y1={0} x2={5} y2={0} label="1Ω" />
  <ElecWire x1={5} y1={0} x2={7} y2={0} />
  <ElecNode x={0} y={0} label="A" />
  <ElecNode x={7} y={0} label="B" />
</FigScene>

components.name: emphasis
components.description: Text emphasis components for bold (strong) and italic (em) styling
components.whenToUse: Use Strong for bold emphasis on critical words. Use Em for softer, tonal emphasis.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not emphasize entire sentences. Do not use emphasis for headings or labels.
components.example: This is critically important and subtly different.

components.name: file-tree
components.description: Simple string-based file/folder tree structure with minimal syntax
components.whenToUse: Use to show project structure or directory layout using an indented string format.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for navigation. Do not exceed 3 levels of nesting without strong reason.
components.example:
<FileTree>
  src/
    components/
      accordion.tsx
    lib/
      utils.ts
  package.json
</FileTree>

components.name: geometry-2d
components.description: 2D geometry visualization components — FigScene (SVG coordinate plane with grid/axes), FigPoint, FigVector, FigLine, FigSegment, FigCircle, FigArc, FigAngle, FigPolygon, FigLabel. All primitives use world coordinates; FigScene handles the pixel transform.
components.whenToUse: Use to draw math geometry diagrams in MDX — coordinate planes, vectors, triangles, angle sweeps, circles. Compose Fig* children inside FigScene. No extra dependencies.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for abstract flow diagrams or data structure visualizations — use Mermaid or ds components. Do not use for inline math symbols — use math-primitives.
components.example:
<FigScene width={400} height={300} xRange={[-5,5]} yRange={[-5,5]}>
  <FigVector toX={3} toY={4} label="v" />
  <FigPoint x={3} y={4} label="A" />
  <FigCircle cx={0} cy={0} r={5} dashed />
</FigScene>

components.name: glossary
components.description: Context-based inline glossary — wrap content in GlossaryProvider with term definitions, then use <Term id> to link and display popovers
components.whenToUse: Use when a document introduces multiple domain-specific terms. Wrap the page in GlossaryProvider and mark first occurrences with Term.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for a single term — use Annotation instead. Do not use GlossaryProvider when fewer than three terms need defining.
components.example:
<GlossaryProvider terms={{ bfs: { label: 'BFS', definition: 'Breadth-First Search: explores nodes level by level.' } }}>
  <Term id="bfs" /> is optimal for shortest-path problems.
</GlossaryProvider>

components.name: hardware-spec
components.description: Structured hardware interface specification card — displays interface parameters (type, version, speed, voltage, pins, protocol) as a compact key-value grid
components.whenToUse: Use when documenting a single hardware interface such as USB, PCIe, I2C, UART, or SPI. Ideal for ICD (Interface Control Document) style reference pages.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for comparing multiple interfaces — use a table instead. Not for software API documentation — use prose with CodeBlock.
components.example:
<HardwareSpec
  name="USB 3.1 Gen 2"
  type="Universal Serial Bus"
  speed="10 Gbps"
  voltage="3.3V / 1.8V"
  pins={24}
  description="High-speed USB for peripheral connectivity."
/>

components.name: heading
components.description: Flexible and reusable heading component with variant support
components.whenToUse: Use H1 for page title, H2 for major sections, H3 for subsections. Maintain strict heading hierarchy.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not skip heading levels. Do not use headings for visual emphasis — use typography components.
components.example:
Algorithm Analysis

Time Complexity

components.name: headings
components.description: Markdown headings (H1-H6) with auto-generated anchor links for navigation
components.whenToUse: Use as the MDX mapping for standard markdown headings when auto-generated anchor links are needed.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use directly in MDX — configure as the h1–h6 override in your MDX components map.
components.example:
// In mdx-components.tsx
{ h1: Headings.H1, h2: Headings.H2 }

components.name: highlight
components.description: Marker-style inline text highlight — simulates a real highlighter pen with yellow, blue, green, pink, or purple colours
components.whenToUse: Use to draw attention to a specific word or phrase in prose, simulating a physical highlighter pen.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for code — use inline-code. Do not highlight entire sentences.
components.example: The key insight is the <Highlight color="yellow">pivot selection strategy</Highlight>.

components.name: horizontal-rule
components.description: Divider lines with multiple styles (default, dashed, dotted, gradient)
components.whenToUse: Use to visually separate major sections within a page when a heading would be too heavy.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use between every paragraph. Do not use as a substitute for proper heading structure.
components.example: <HorizontalRule variant="gradient" />

components.name: image
components.description: Image with optional caption, plus ImageGlossary — responsive 1–3 column grid of images as seen in react.dev
components.whenToUse: Use for screenshots, diagrams, and illustrations with an optional caption. Use ImageGlossary for a grid of related images.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for decorative images with no informational value. Always provide meaningful alt text.
components.example: <Image src="/diagrams/avl-rotation.png" alt="AVL tree left rotation" caption="Left rotation restores balance when right subtree is too tall." />

components.name: inline-code
components.description: Inline code component for displaying code snippets within text
components.whenToUse: Use for inline code mentions — variable names, function names, short snippets within prose.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for multi-line code — use CodeBlock. Do not use for keyboard shortcuts — use Kbd.
components.example: Call <InlineCode>Array.prototype.reduce</InlineCode> with an initial accumulator.

components.name: invariant
components.description: Formal invariant statement — highlights a condition that must always hold, with optional complexity annotation
components.whenToUse: Use to state a formal invariant — a condition that must always hold. Particularly valuable in algorithm and data structure documentation.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for general facts or notes — use Callout. Reserve for formal correctness claims with optional complexity annotation.
components.example:
<Invariant complexity="O(log n)">
  An AVL tree remains height-balanced after every insertion and deletion.
</Invariant>

components.name: json-ld
components.description: JSON-LD structured data — renders a <script type='application/ld+json'> tag for SEO (Article, BreadcrumbList, FAQPage, etc.)
components.whenToUse: Use to embed structured data for SEO — Article, BreadcrumbList, FAQPage schemas.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for visible content. This component renders no visible output.
components.example: <JsonLd schema={{ '@context': 'https://schema.org', '@type': 'Article', headline: 'Binary Search Trees' }} />

components.name: kbd
components.description: Keyboard shortcut display — styled <kbd> element for showing key combinations like Ctrl+K
components.whenToUse: Use to show keyboard shortcuts and key combinations inline with instructional text.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for code — use InlineCode. Do not use for mouse actions.
components.example: Press <Kbd>Ctrl</Kbd>+<Kbd>K</Kbd> to open the command palette.

components.name: link
components.description: Styled anchor — auto-detects external URLs, adds open-in-new-tab icon and rel='noopener noreferrer'
components.whenToUse: Use for all anchor links in MDX. It auto-detects external URLs and adds rel='noopener noreferrer' and an external icon automatically.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for navigation buttons or actions — use a button element.
components.example: <Link href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AVL_tree">AVL tree</Link>

components.name: list
components.description: Styled ordered and unordered lists with list items
components.whenToUse: Use UnorderedList for items with no required order. Use OrderedList when sequence matters.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for step-by-step procedures — use Steps. Do not nest more than two levels.
components.example:
<UnorderedList>
  <ListItem>O(1) average case lookup</ListItem>
  <ListItem>O(n) worst case with hash collision</ListItem>
</UnorderedList>

components.name: math-equation
components.description: Display equation containers: Equation (centered block with optional equation number label) and EqSystem (system of simultaneous equations with SVG left brace). Relation symbols (Approx, Neq, Leq, Geq, Arrow, Implies, Iff, etc.) live in math-primitives.
components.whenToUse: Use Equation to display a formula prominently with an optional reference number — pair with <M expr="..." /> for LaTeX content or math-primitives for JSX layout. Use EqSystem for systems of simultaneous equations.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for prose paragraphs that mention variables — use M or InlineMath. Do not use for code listings.

components.name: math-primitives
components.description: 150+ JSX math primitive components across 11 sections: Basic (Frac, Pow, Sub, Sqrt, Abs, Paren, Deg, Inf), Calculus (Integral, Sum, Prod, Lim, Limsup, Liminf, Deriv, PDeriv, Nabla, Laplacian), Trig (Sin–Csc, ArcSin–ArcTan, Sinh/Cosh/Tanh, Log/Ln/Exp), Algebra (Factorial, Choose, Perm, Mod, GCD, LCM), Set Theory (Floor, Ceil, SetOf, Cardinality, PowerSet, In, Subset, Union, Intersect…), Number Systems (NN ℕ, ZZ ℤ, QQ ℚ, RR ℝ, CC ℂ, PP ℙ, FF 𝔽, Complex, Conj), Logic (And, Or, Not, ForAll, Exists, Implies ⟹, Iff ⟺, Therefore, QED), Linear Algebra (Vec, Norm, Dot, Cross, Transpose, Det, Matrix, Rank, Dim, Trace), Statistics (Prob, CondProb, Expected, Variance, StdDev, Cov, Corr, Dist), Greek (full set), Relations/Arrows (Neq, Approx, Equiv, Cong, Leq, Geq, Ll, Gg, Propto, Sim, PlusMinus, MinusPlus, Divides, NotDivides, Arrow, MapsTo, Compose, OTimes).
components.whenToUse: Use for structured math UI — numbered equations with layout, solution steps, inline symbols alongside prose. For raw LaTeX expressions, use <M expr="..." /> instead — it is simpler. Both approaches can be mixed freely.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use primitives to write raw math expressions — use <M expr="\frac{a}{b}" /> for that. Primitives are for composing layout-level math components.
components.example: <Equation label="1"><Frac num={<>-b <PlusMinus /> <Sqrt>b²-4ac</Sqrt></>} den="2a" /></Equation>

components.name: math-solution
components.description: Step-by-step mathematical solution blocks: Solution (wrapper with title), SolutionStep (one transformation step with reason), SolutionAnswer (final result with ∴ badge), SolutionNote (commentary annotation)
components.whenToUse: Use to walk through algebraic derivations, equation solving, or proofs step by step. Each step shows the expression and an optional reason.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for general procedure guides — use Steps/Step for non-math workflows. Do not use for code walkthroughs.
components.example:
<Solution title="Solve: 2x + 4 = 10">
  <SolutionStep reason="Given">2x + 4 = 10</SolutionStep>
  <SolutionStep reason="Subtract 4">2x = 6</SolutionStep>
  <SolutionAnswer>x = 3</SolutionAnswer>
</Solution>

components.name: mdx-components
components.description: Auto-discovering MDX component mapper - automatically uses all installed DocsUI components
components.whenToUse: Use as the central MDX component map for your docs site. Import and register all DocsUI components here.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not import this file directly in MDX — it is a configuration file for your MDX renderer.
components.example:
// mdx-components.tsx
export const components = { Callout, Steps, Reveal, ... }

components.name: mermaid
components.description: Mermaid diagram renderer with auto type-detection, per-type label headers, and data structure components — MermaidBST, MermaidTree, MermaidBFS, MermaidDFS
components.whenToUse: Use for flowcharts, sequence diagrams, state machines, and general diagrams where relationships and flow matter.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for data structure visualization — use DS components. Do not use for complex graphs with many nodes — prefer DSGraph.
components.example:
<Mermaid chart={`graph TD
  A[Start] --> B{Is balanced?}
  B -->|Yes| C[Return]
  B -->|No| D[Rotate]
  D --> B`} />

components.name: paragraph
components.description: Standard text paragraph component with proper spacing and typography
components.whenToUse: Use as the MDX mapping for standard prose paragraphs.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use directly in MDX — configure as the p override in your MDX components map.
components.example:
// In mdx-components.tsx
{ p: Paragraph }

components.name: pin-table
components.description: Hardware pinout table — documents connector pins with signal name, direction type (INPUT/OUTPUT/BIDIR/POWER/GND/NC), voltage, alternate function, and description. Color-coded type badges.
components.whenToUse: Use when documenting a microcontroller, SoC, or connector pinout — USB, GPIO, UART, SPI, I2C headers. Ideal for hardware BSP and SDK documentation.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for register maps — use RegisterMap. Do not use for general comparison tables — use DataTable.
components.example:
<PinTable title="40-pin GPIO Header" connector="J1" rows={[
  { pin: 1, signal: "3.3V", type: "POWER", voltage: "3.3V", description: "Power supply" },
  { pin: 2, signal: "5V",   type: "POWER", voltage: "5V",   description: "Power supply" },
  { pin: 3, signal: "GPIO2", type: "BIDIR", voltage: "3.3V", altFunction: "I2C1_SDA" },
]} />

components.name: preview
components.description: Tabbed component preview — shows rendered output and source code side by side with a copy button
components.whenToUse: Use to show a rendered component alongside its source code. Best for component documentation pages.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-visual components or when source code alone is sufficient. Do not use for interactive demos requiring live editing — that needs a sandbox.
components.example:
<Preview lang="tsx" code={`<Badge variant="success">Stable</Badge>`}>
  <Badge variant="success">Stable</Badge>
</Preview>

components.name: privacy-table
components.description: Personal data collection documentation table — records data type, purpose, legal basis (GDPR), retention period, and third-party sharing status
components.whenToUse: Use inside Privacy Policy pages or Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) to document what personal data is collected and why. Supports ISO 27001 and TISAX compliance documentation.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for hardware or API specs — use RegisterMap or DataTable instead. Not for displaying non-privacy data.
components.example:
<PrivacyTable
  rows={[
    { dataType: "Email Address",       purpose: "Authentication",    legalBasis: "Consent",              retention: "Until deletion", shared: false },
    { dataType: "Chipset Serial Number", purpose: "Device diagnostics", legalBasis: "Legitimate Interest",   retention: "5 years",        shared: true  },
  ]}
  caption="Per Qualcomm Privacy Policy"
/>

components.name: register-map
components.description: Hardware register or OTP fuse map table — documents memory-mapped registers with address, name, bit range, access type (RO/WO/RW/OTP/RC), reset value, and description
components.whenToUse: Use for documenting MMIO registers, QFPROM fuse maps, CSR tables, or any bit-addressable hardware configuration in SDK or BSP documentation.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for software API parameters — use prose or CodeBlock. Not for data privacy records — use PrivacyTable.
components.example:
<RegisterMap
  title="QFPROM Fuse Map — Security Control"
  rows={[
    { address: "0x00780000", name: "QFPROM_CORR_RD_WR_PERM_LSB", bits: "31:0", access: "OTP", reset: "0x00000000", description: "Read/write permissions for fuse rows." },
    { address: "0x00780008", name: "QFPROM_CORR_JTAG_ID",        bits: "31:0", access: "RO",  reset: "0x009600E1", description: "JTAG identification register." },
  ]}
/>

components.name: reveal
components.description: Animated click-to-reveal panel for answers, hints, and spoilers — uses smooth Collapse animation
components.whenToUse: Use to hide a single answer, solution, or hint the reader must actively choose to reveal. Common in tutorials and exercises.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for navigation or multi-section disclosure — use Accordion. Do not use when content should always be visible.
components.example:
<Reveal label="Show solution">
  Use a hash map keyed on the complement: `target - nums[i]`.
</Reveal>

components.name: security-note
components.description: Security-specific callout with severity levels (info, warning, critical) — uses a lock/shield icon and colour-coded treatment distinct from general Callout
components.whenToUse: Use for security notices, OTP fuse burn caveats, key management constraints, or vulnerability disclosures. Choose severity: info for general notes, warning for important caveats, critical for irreversible or dangerous actions.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for general tips or non-security content — use Callout instead. Do not use critical severity for minor warnings.
components.example:
<SecurityNote severity="warning" title="OTP Fuse Burn">
  QFPROM fuse values are permanent once written. Ensure correct values before provisioning production devices.
</SecurityNote>

components.name: spoiler
components.description: Collapsible <details>/<summary> disclosure — lightweight alternative to Accordion for single items
components.whenToUse: Use for lightweight single-item disclosure using native HTML details/summary when animation is not needed.
components.whenNotToUse: Use Reveal instead when animation matters. Do not use for multiple related items — use Accordion.
components.example:
<Spoiler summary="What is the time complexity?">
  O(log n) for a balanced BST.
</Spoiler>

components.name: steps
components.description: Numbered step-by-step guides with visual indicators
components.whenToUse: Use for sequential procedures where order matters — installation, configuration, tutorials.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for unordered tips — use a list. Do not use when steps can be performed in any order.
components.example:
<Steps>
  <Step title="Install">`pnpm add docsui-cli`</Step>
  <Step title="Run">`npx docsui-cli@latest add callout`</Step>
</Steps>

components.name: table
components.description: Styled table components with header, body, footer, and caption
components.whenToUse: Use for static reference tables with fewer than 10 rows — prop reference, comparison tables, structured data.
components.whenNotToUse: Use DataTable instead for dynamic data, sorting, or filtering.
components.example: OperationComplexitySearchO(log n)

components.name: tabs
components.description: Tabbed content sections with state management and keyboard navigation
components.whenToUse: Use to show mutually exclusive content variants — framework-specific code, OS instructions, or alternative approaches.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for sequential content — use Steps. Do not use more than five tabs.
components.example:
<Tabs>
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="ts">TypeScript</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="js">JavaScript</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="ts">const x: number = 1</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="js">const x = 1</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

components.name: terminal
components.description: Terminal window component — macOS-style title bar with traffic lights, TerminalLine for input/output lines
components.whenToUse: Use to show command-line sessions with a macOS-style title bar. Use TerminalLine for input/output lines.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for code files — use CodeBlock. Use for interactive shell sessions only.
components.example:
<Terminal>
  <TerminalLine type="input">pnpm add docsui-cli</TerminalLine>
  <TerminalLine type="output">+ @docsui-cli/cli 0.0.33</TerminalLine>
</Terminal>

components.name: tree
components.description: Interactive file/folder tree structure for displaying project organization
components.whenToUse: Use to show hierarchical structures — project layout, category trees, nested navigation.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for navigation menus. Do not use for more than three levels of nesting without strong reason.
components.example:
<Tree>
  <TreeItem label="src">
    <TreeItem label="components" />
    <TreeItem label="lib" />
  </TreeItem>
</Tree>

components.name: utils
components.description: Utility functions for className merging (cn)
components.whenToUse: Install automatically as a dependency of any other component. Contains the cn() class merging utility.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not import directly in MDX files. This is an internal utility.
components.example: import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'

components.name: video
components.description: Video embed — auto-detects YouTube, Vimeo, and HTML5 video sources with aspect-ratio container and optional caption
components.whenToUse: Use to embed YouTube, Vimeo, or HTML5 video with an aspect-ratio container and optional caption.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not use for audio-only content. Do not embed auto-playing videos.
components.example: <Video src="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ" caption="Algorithm walkthrough" />
